description_long: This sculpture features two abstracted, stylized human figures rendered in a smooth, stone-like material, positioned on a square plinth. One figure is seated with its head resting on its hand, while the other is bent forward, almost kneeling, with its arms and head close to the ground. The forms are rounded and simplified, lacking detailed facial features or fingers, emphasizing the overall posture and relationship between the figures. The base is flat and rectangular, with the figures integrated into its surface, suggesting a contemplative or intimate scene. The surface of the sculpture appears weathered and matte, with subtle variations in texture and color. The entire piece exudes a sense of quiet interaction and abstraction, suitable for public display in an urban or park setting.
description: An abstract stone sculpture of two stylized human figures on a square plinth, with smooth, rounded forms and a contemplative posture.
description_view_0: From this angle, both figures are visible: one is seated with its head resting on its hand, while the other is bent forward, almost kneeling.
description_view_1: This view highlights the reclining figure's arm and the curvature of the bent figure, emphasizing their interaction and the negative space between them.
description_view_2: Seen from above and behind, the simplified forms and the integration of the figures into the plinth are most apparent.
description_view_3: This angle shows the back of the bent figure and the side of the seated figure, with the abstracted limbs and rounded forms clearly visible.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is set on a flat, concrete-like plinth, suggesting an outdoor urban or park environment.
